steps.title: Solve for the needed coefficient
steps.content: For an equation in $x$ to have no solutions, the $x$-coefficients must match but the constants must not match. Expanding the right side gives $$9x+5=ax+ab.$$ Matching the $x$-coefficients gives $a=9$, so statement I must be true.

steps.title: Find the excluded value of b
steps.content: With $a=9$, the equation becomes $9x+5=9x+9b$. If $5=9b$, the equation would have infinitely many solutions instead of no solutions. Therefore no solutions require $$9b\neq5\Rightarrow b\neq\frac{5}{9}.$$

steps.title: Translate the condition
steps.content: The condition is exactly $$a=9\quad\text{and}\quad b\neq\frac{5}{9}.$$ That means $a$ must equal $9$, while $b$ only has to avoid one value.

steps.title: Select the answer
steps.content: Statement I is required. Statement II is not required because $b$ can be any value except $\frac{5}{9}$. Statement III is required. Therefore only I and III must be true, which matches the correct answer.
